parentQuestion: A 28-year-old woman presents with hyperextensible joints (she can bend her thumb back to touch her forearm), thin skin that bruises easily, and poor wound healing with 'cigarette-paper' scars. Which protein is most likely defective?

scaffolds.q: COLLAGEN is the main structural protein that provides strength to connective tissues.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Collagen fibers resist stretching and provide tensile strength to skin, ligaments, and joints.

scaffolds.q: Ligaments connect bone to bone and stabilize joints.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Ligaments are made mostly of collagen. Weak collagen = weak ligaments = unstable joints.

scaffolds.q: If collagen is defective, joints can become TOO flexible (hyperextensible).
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Normal collagen limits how far joints can bend. Defective collagen allows excessive movement.

scaffolds.q: Skin contains collagen in the dermis layer.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Collagen gives skin its strength and resistance to tearing. Weak collagen = fragile skin.

scaffolds.q: Wound healing requires new collagen to form scar tissue.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! If the new collagen is defective, scars will be thin and weak - like 'cigarette paper.'

scaffolds.q: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome is a genetic disorder affecting collagen.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! EDS involves mutations in collagen genes, causing stretchy joints, fragile skin, and poor healing.

scaffolds.q: Elastin problems would cause loose, sagging skin - NOT hyperextensible joints.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Elastin provides elastic recoil. Collagen provides tensile strength. Different proteins, different problems.

scaffolds.q: This woman's symptoms - hyperflexible joints, fragile skin, thin scars - point to COLLAGEN defect.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! These are classic Ehlers-Danlos syndrome features caused by defective collagen.

scaffolds.q: The answer is COLLAGEN - defective collagen causes hyperextensible joints and fragile skin.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Not elastin (would cause sagging), not keratin (affects hair/nails/skin surface). Collagen = structural integrity.
